text.title: devops-aws-three-tier Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for devops-aws-three-tier. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Production VPCs MUST span at least 2 AZs for each subnet tier (public, private, database). Single-AZ deployments are forbidden for prod. NAT Gateways MUST be one per AZ in prod and one shared for dev/staging.
rule.rationale: AZ failure takes down all resources in that AZ simultaneously. A single NAT Gateway is an AZ-level SPOF that blocks all outbound traffic from private subnets if the AZ fails.

rule.statement: ALB listeners MUST redirect HTTP 80 to HTTPS 443 with status 301. SSL policy MUST be ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS13-1-2-2021-06 or newer. ALB access logs MUST be enabled.
rule.rationale: HTTP-to-HTTPS redirect at the load balancer prevents plaintext traffic from reaching the app. TLS 1.3-only policy drops broken legacy ciphers. Access logs are required for forensics and compliance.

rule.statement: Aurora clusters MUST enable storage_encrypted with a customer-managed KMS key, enable performance_insights, set deletion_protection=true in prod, and use iam_database_authentication_enabled=true. Backup retention MUST be ≥30 days in prod.
rule.rationale: Customer-managed KMS enables key revocation for compliance. IAM authentication eliminates static DB passwords. Deletion protection prevents accidental data loss from a Terraform mistake.
